// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance CranePositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Continuously captures and logs the crane's position data for real-time monitoring and validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CranePositionDataTransmitted
]

instance CraneVelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures and transmits the crane's velocity data in real-time for trajectory validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneVelocityDataTransmitted
]

instance CraneAccelerationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Records acceleration data of the crane to validate dynamic behavior against simulated models."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneAccelerationDataTransmitted
]

instance CraneOperationalStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors the operational state of the crane (e.g., active/idle, fault conditions)."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneOperationalStatusDataTransmitted
]

instance PuckPositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Tracks the position of the crane's puck (load) for trajectory validation and kinematic analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PuckPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance KinematicStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Captures the crane's kinematic states (e.g., joint angles, velocities) for real-time validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData KinematicStateDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance CranePositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CranePositionSensor
]

instance CraneVelocityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneVelocitySensor
]

instance CraneAccelerationDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneAccelerationSensor
]

instance CraneOperationalStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneOperationalStatusSensor
]

instance PuckPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PuckPositionSensor
]

instance KinematicStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom KinematicStateSensor
]

instance TrajectoryExecutionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PuckPositionSensor, CranePositionSensor, CraneVelocitySensor
    base:desc "Real-time measurements of executed trajectories including actual crane movements and puck positions."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance TimescaleDBHistoricalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Centralized repository storing time-series operational data (e.g., position, velocity, acceleration, status) from the Logging Application."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CranePositionDataTransmitted, CraneVelocityDataTransmitted, CraneAccelerationDataTransmitted, CraneOperationalStatusDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBEnabler
]

instance SimulatedTrajectoryModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Mathematical model representing optimal crane trajectories generated by the Trajectory Generator Service."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TrajectoryGeneratorEnabler
]

instance PhysicalConstraintModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Replicates physical constraints of the crane (e.g., mechanical limits, actuator capabilities) for validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationServiceEnabler
]

instance ExecutedTrajectoryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time data of executed trajectories captured by the Continuous Validation Service for comparison with simulated trajectories."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TrajectoryExecutionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ContinuousValidationEnabler
]

instance ErrorMetricData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Calculated distance metrics (e.g., Euclidean or kinematic error) between executed and simulated trajectories."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ExecutedTrajectoryData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ContinuousValidationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance TimescaleDBEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database optimized for high-frequency crane operational data storage and retrieval."
    DTDFVocab:enables LoggingService
]

instance MQTTBrokerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lightweight, event-driven message broker facilitating asynchronous communication between services (e.g., Logging Application, Trajectory Generator, Simulation Service)."
    DTDFVocab:enables LoggingService, TrajectoryGeneratorService, SimulationService, ContinuousValidationService
]

instance TrajectoryGeneratorEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Preprocesses kinematic models to generate optimal trajectories for crane operations."
    DTDFVocab:enables TrajectoryGeneratorService
]

instance SimulationServiceEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Executes simulations replicating physical constraints for validation against real-world data."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationService
]

instance ContinuousValidationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enforces real-time comparison of executed vs. simulated trajectories and calculates error metrics."
    DTDFVocab:enables ContinuousValidationService
]

instance GrafanaDashboardEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Visualization platform aggregating and displaying integrated data for monitoring and operator intervention."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance LoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuously captures and logs the crane's state (position, velocity, acceleration, operational status) into TimescaleDB."
    DTDFVocab:provides CraneStateInsight
]

instance TrajectoryGeneratorService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generates optimal trajectories for crane operations based on kinematic models and physical constraints."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimalTrajectoryInsight
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulates crane operations under physical constraints for validation and testing."
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationResultInsight
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Validates digital twin accuracy by comparing simulated and executed trajectories, triggering alerts for discrepancies."
    DTDFVocab:provides ValidationAlertAction, ErrorMetricInsight
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides interactive dashboards for monitoring crane operations, kinematic models, and system states."
    DTDFVocab:provides CranePerformanceInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance CraneStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time and historical operational state of the crane (position, velocity, acceleration, status)."
]

instance OptimalTrajectoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized trajectories for crane movements based on kinematic and physical constraint models."
]

instance SimulationResultInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Results of simulated crane operations under physical constraints for validation."
]

instance ErrorMetricInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Calculated distance metrics (e.g., Euclidean or kinematic error) between executed and simulated trajectories."
]

instance ValidationAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Triggers alerts via Grafana Dashboard when error thresholds are breached."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance CranePerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualized performance metrics of the crane, including trajectory accuracy and operational efficiency."
]

// C1: Physical Components
instance PhysicalCrane : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Small-scale gantry crane system with mechanical structures (beams, pulleys, hooks), actuators (motors), and embedded sensors."
    base:isContainedIn LaboratoryEnvironment
]

    base:desc "Embedded or programmable logic controller (PLC) managing crane operations, motion control, and safety mechanisms."

]

]

// C2: Acting Components
instance CraneActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuators (e.g., motors) responsible for executing crane movements based on controller commands."

]

// C12: Environments
instance LaboratoryEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Controlled laboratory/educational setting for the gantry crane Digital Twin system."
    base:contains PhysicalCrane, CraneController, CraneActuator, PhysicalCraneInterface
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in the Digital Twin system is mediated by the **Control Application** and **Trajectory Generator Service**, which translate digital commands into physical actions for the **Physical Crane**. Key details include:
  - **Command Types**: The **Trajectory Generator Service** generates optimal movement trajectories (e.g., Cartesian or joint-space paths) with no residual swinging motion at the end. These trajectories are likely parameterized commands (e.g., target positions, velocities, accelerations) for the crane’s controllers.
  - **Control Message Formats**: Commands are transmitted via the **Physical Crane Interface** (Python-based) to the crane’s controllers. The exact format is unspecified, but the **Control Application** likely uses structured data (e.g., JSON, protobuf) to encode trajectory parameters.
  - **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**: Not explicitly described, but the **Continuous Validation Service** implicitly validates command execution by comparing simulated vs. actual trajectories. Operator authentication may occur via the **Human Machine Interface (HMI)** for critical actions.
  - **Feedback Systems**: The **Logging Application** provides real-time feedback on crane state (e.g., position, velocity) to the **Control Application** and **Dashboard**. The **Continuous Validation Service** offers post-execution feedback by highlighting discrepancies between planned and executed trajectories.
  - **Conflict Resolution**: Not explicitly detailed, but the **Control Application** likely prioritizes commands based on system state (e.g., emergency stops override trajectory commands). The **MQTT Broker** may handle asynchronous conflict resolution via message queuing.
  - **Command Execution Confirmation**: The **Continuous Validation Service** confirms execution by comparing simulated and actual trajectories. Alerts are triggered via the **Dashboard** if thresholds are breached, enabling corrective actions."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin systems described adopt a **lightweight, ad-hoc orchestration approach** rather than relying on commercial frameworks. The **GreenhouseDT** and **Air Quality Management** exemplars leverage **containerization/virtualization** (e.g., virtual machines with prerecorded data for GreenhouseDT) and **single-board computers (Raspberry Pi)** for localized execution, indicating a **microservices-like constellation** with minimal system-wide coordination. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar uses **Microsoft Azure tools** for pipeline implementation, suggesting **cloud-based orchestration** for runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction services, but without explicit details on resource management or integration patterns. The **gantry crane system** (described via C4 Model) implies a **modular, component-based architecture** with potential **event-driven coordination** (e.g., container movement triggers), though no specific orchestration framework (e.g., Kubernetes, Apache Kafka) is mentioned. The **lack of a commercial digital twin framework** in all exemplars points to **custom, domain-specific orchestration** with **manual or scripted integration** between physical and digital components."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar emphasizes **self-adaptive behavioral models** with **prerecorded data** in virtual machines to ensure consistency between the physical and digital systems. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar employs **Microsoft Azure tools** for **simulation and prediction**, implying validation through **runtime visualization** and **model-driven engineering** methodologies. **Uncertainty sources** are not explicitly quantified, but the **ad-hoc approach** suggests reliance on **sensor accuracy** (e.g., Raspberry Pi-based sensors) and **AutomationML's structural fidelity**. **Error detection** mechanisms are not detailed, though the use of **InfluxDB** and **ActiveMQ** implies real-time data reconciliation. **Quality assurance** processes are inferred from the **GitHub-hosted documentation** and **containerization/virtualization** for reproducible deployments."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin’s Technical Connection** infrastructure relies on a **service-oriented architecture** with the following technical details:
  - **Communication Protocols**:
    - **MQTT**: Used for lightweight, event-driven signaling between services (e.g., **Logging Application**, **Trajectory Generator Service**, **Simulation Service**). MQTT’s publish-subscribe model ensures decoupled communication.
    - **Database Protocols**: **TimescaleDB** (PostgreSQL-based) supports time-series data storage and querying, with likely HTTP/REST or custom protocols for service integration.
    - **Crane Controller Interface**: The **Physical Crane Interface** (Python) connects to the crane’s controllers, though the protocol (e.g., Modbus, OPC UA, proprietary) is unspecified.
  - **Network Topology and Architecture**: The system follows a **microservices architecture** with containers (abstractions per C4 Model) communicating via the **MQTT Broker**. The **Database** acts as a central data store, while services (e.g., **Trajectory Generator**, **Simulation Service**) operate independently.
  - **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
    - **Trajectory Validation**: Low-latency (<100ms) required for real-time comparison of executed vs. simulated trajectories by the **Continuous Validation Service**.
    - **Logging**: High-throughput but tolerates slightly higher latency for historical data storage in **TimescaleDB**.
    - **MQTT**: Optimized for low-bandwidth, high-frequency messaging (e.g., crane state updates).
  - **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**: Not explicitly described, but **TimescaleDB** supports high availability and fault tolerance for critical data. The **MQTT Broker** may include QoS levels (e.g., QoS 1 or 2) for message reliability.
  - **Security Protocols**: Not detailed, but implied security measures include:
    - **Authentication**: Likely enforced via the **Human Machine Interface** for operator access.
    - **Data Integrity**: **TimescaleDB** ensures data consistency, while the **Continuous Validation Service** validates trajectory execution integrity.
    - **Network Security**: Assumed to include standard practices (e.g., TLS for MQTT, firewall rules for service isolation)."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin’s Hosting and Deployment** infrastructure is designed as a **hybrid edge-on-premise system** with the following specifications:
  - **Hosting Platforms**:
    - **Edge Deployment**: The **Physical Crane Interface**, **Control Application**, **Logging Application**, and **Human Machine Interface** likely run on-premise (co-located with the crane) to minimize latency for real-time control and monitoring.
    - **On-Premise Services**: The **Database (TimescaleDB)**, **MQTT Broker**, **Trajectory Generator Service**, **Simulation Service**, and **Continuous Validation Service** are deployed on local servers or workstations to ensure deterministic performance for trajectory planning and validation.
    - **Cloud Consideration**: Not explicitly mentioned, but **TimescaleDB** and **Grafana Dashboard** could theoretically be hosted in a private cloud for scalability, though the system prioritizes low-latency edge processing.
  - **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
    - **CPU/GPU**: The **Trajectory Generator Service** and **Simulation Service** require computational resources for kinematic modeling and physics-based simulations (e.g., rigid-body dynamics). The **Continuous Validation Service** demands real-time processing for trajectory comparison.
    - **Storage**: **TimescaleDB** stores time-series data (e.g., crane states, trajectory logs) with high write throughput. Storage scalability depends on logging frequency and retention policies.
  - **Scalability Mechanisms**:
    - **Horizontal Scaling**: The **MQTT Broker** and **Database** can scale horizontally to handle increased message loads or data volume.
    - **Service Isolation**: Containers (per C4 Model) allow independent scaling of services (e.g., deploying multiple instances of the **Simulation Service** for parallel validation).
  - **Deployment Models Supported**:
    - **Containerized Deployment**: Services are abstracted as containers (e.g., Docker-like environments) for portability and isolation.
    - **Monolithic Alternative**: The **Physical Crane System** can operate without the Digital Twin, implying a fallback to standalone control.
  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
    - **High Availability**: **TimescaleDB** and critical services (e.g., **MQTT Broker**) are likely configured for redundancy.
    - **Fault Tolerance**: The **Continuous Validation Service** includes threshold-based alerts to notify operators of anomalies, enabling manual or automated recovery."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin systems exhibit **limited horizontal integration** with external systems, relying on **ad-hoc or domain-specific protocols**. The **GreenhouseDT** integrates with **InfluxDB** (time-series database) and **ActiveMQ** (message broker) for internal data exchange, but no external system dependencies (e.g., ERP, IoT platforms) are documented. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar uses **AutomationML** for digital twin modeling and **Microsoft Azure tools** for pipeline execution, implying **standardized data exchange** within the construction domain but no explicit interoperability with other Digital Twins or enterprise systems. The **gantry crane system** (though partially described) suggests **cyber-physical system (CPS) integration** (e.g., sensor networks, logistics software) but lacks technical details on protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) or standards. All exemplars avoid **commercial frameworks**, indicating **proprietary or custom integration patterns** with minimal external dependencies. The **lack of explicit interoperability standards** (e.g., FIWARE, OMG DDS) further restricts horizontal scalability."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Air Quality Management exemplar adheres to:
  - **AutomationML** for digital twin modeling and interoperability.
  - **Microsoft Azure tools** (likely compliant with cloud/industry standards for data processing and analytics).
  The GreenhouseDT exemplar uses:
  - **InfluxDB** (time-series database, no explicit standardization mentioned).
  - **ActiveMQ** (message broker, no standardization details).
  The gantry crane exemplar does not reference any standards. No certification requirements, compliance frameworks (e.g., ISO 22400 for digital twins), or interoperability protocols beyond AutomationML are documented."
]
